3.name: Adze
flags: elemental_affinity:None (Extraplanar -- summoned from the Dómnathar's home plane)
3.environment: Deepwater Marches (halfling settlements, marsh villages, river-trading posts)
3.description: The lamp at the window gutters. A firefly drifts in through the gap beneath the door -- just one, glowing faintly amber in the dark. It lands on the sleeping child's arm. By morning, the child is pale, feverish, and will not wake. The firefly is gone. There is no wound to find.

3.name: Ankheg
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth)
3.environment: The Eternal Steppe (cultivated farmland and open grassland, especially along the Kinsweave border); secondary presence in Twin Rivers (tilled lowland fields near the Great River) and Tidebloom Reach (drained marshland converted to cropland near Hillmeet)
3.description: The wheat shivers and collapses in a line, stalks folding inward like a wound stitching itself shut. Then the earth cracks open and something huge and chitinous erupts -- six legs driving it skyward, mandibles gaping, a hiss of acrid vapor rising from its mouth. It is the color of the soil it lives in, which means you never see it until the ground beneath you gives way.

3.name: Assassin Vine
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dual affinity reflecting root-depth and water dependence
3.environment: Great Forests (Deepwood, Twin Rivers old-growth, dense undergrowth near streams and springs); secondary presence in Tidebloom Reach (tidal forests) and Deepwater Marches (jungle undergrowth)
3.description: The trail narrows between two stands of old-growth trees, their canopy so thick that the forest floor is dim even at midday. Moss covers everything -- the tree trunks, the stones, the trail itself. There is no wind, but something moves at the edge of your vision: a vine, thick as a man's forearm, sliding across the path with a slow, purposeful motion that has nothing to do with the breeze. By the time you notice that the vines are moving on every side of you, they are already too close.

3.name: Centaur
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- dual affinity; earth-rooted but wind-spirited
3.environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transition zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland, particularly along the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier and the western forest-edge near the Twin Rivers)
3.organization: Solitary, company (5-8), troop (8-18 plus 1 leader of 2nd-5th level), or herd (20-150 adults plus 30% noncombatants plus 10 3rd-level sergeants plus 5 5th-level lieutenants plus 1 7th-level leader)
3.description: It comes out of the tree-line at a canter -- the lower body of a heavy steppe-horse, the upper body of a broad-chested warrior, carrying a horn-and-sinew composite bow that would take a human archer both hands to string. Its eyes scan the grassland with the alert, unhurried confidence of a creature that has never needed to run from anything. The wind shifts, and you realise there are more of them in the trees -- a dozen, perhaps more, watching you with the same calculating patience.

3.name: Doppelganger
flags: elemental_affinity:None native -- doppelgangers are alien to Oreka; see Breach Resonance
3.environment: Any settlement (doppelgangers are urban predators found wherever Kin populations are dense enough to provide cover -- primarily Kinsweave border-kingdoms, Eruskan river-cities, Tidebloom Reach towns, and the larger Pekakarlik oasis-settlements)
3.description: It looks like you. It looks exactly like you -- your face, your build, your clothes (not real, just shaped flesh mimicking fabric), and the way you hold your shoulders when you are tired. It even smells like you, or close enough that a casual observer would not notice. But its thoughts are not your thoughts, and behind the mask of your face, something alien is calculating how long it can wear you before someone notices.

3.name: Dryad
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- root and sap; the dryad is bound to the land and the water that sustains it
3.environment: Great Forests (old-growth oak groves, ancient forest, anywhere a single tree has stood long enough to develop fey resonance -- primarily Hasura territory, Twin Rivers old-growth, and the deeper Pasua forest-margins)
3.description: She is standing inside the oak. Not behind it -- inside it, her body half-merged with the bark, her skin the colour of heartwood, her hair a cascade of leaves that shift with a breeze that does not touch any other tree. Her eyes are green. Not metaphorically -- green the way chlorophyll is green, the green of photosynthesis, the green of something that feeds on light. She watches you with an expression that is not human curiosity. It is the expression of a tree noticing that something is standi...

3.name: Dire Ape
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Air (canopy and trunk -- the Deepwood Paragon Ape is an expression of forest verticality)
3.environment: Deepwood Canopy (the highest, densest sections of the Great Forests, particularly the southern Deepwood where the canopy forms an unbroken ceiling 100+ feet above the forest floor)
3.organization: Solitary or company (2-4, siblings from the same Paragon siring)

3.name: Encantado
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- the encantado is the Lady's domain expressed as charm rather than force
3.environment: Apelian Sea and Deepwater Marches coast (warm coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal lagoons; in dolphin form, ranges across the full Apelian basin; in humanoid form, visits coastal settlements, particularly halfling fishing villages and the smaller Deepwater Marches trading-posts)
3.description: The stranger walks out of the sea at dusk, barefoot on the wet sand, wearing a hat pulled low over their forehead. They are beautiful -- unnaturally, painfully beautiful -- and they move with a liquid grace that makes every step look like swimming. They smile at the fishing village and the fishing village smiles back, because it has been a long time since anyone beautiful came to these shores. The hat stays on. It always stays on. Beneath the hat, the blowhole is still wet.

3.name: Lasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; river-current and willow-shade, the slow music of water over stone
3.environment: Great River (the quiet bends, willow-pools, and sacred confluences of the Great River system -- particularly the Eruskan stretches between Twin Rivers and the Sea Falls, where the river widens and the current slows)
3.organization: Solitary, pair, or confluence (3-8 at a major river junction)
3.description: The river is quieter here -- the current softens where the willow-roots meet the water, and the evening light turns the surface to hammered copper. You see her sitting on the stones at the water's edge, bare feet in the current, combing her hair with a comb made of river-shell. She is not quite solid. The light passes through her edges. When she turns to look at you, her eyes are the colour of deep water, and the song she has been humming settles into your chest like a second heartbeat. The Gr...

3.name: Mud Menders
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- constructive; the intersection of water and earth as building material, the quiet industry of riverbanks repairing themselves
3.environment: Marshes and River Deltas (the Deepwater Marches' mudflats, the Great River's flood-plains, and the tidal estuaries where fresh water meets the Apelian -- anywhere that river-mud accumulates in sufficient depth and elemental water-earth energy is concentrated)
3.description: The riverbank is repairing itself. Where the spring flood tore a section away, leaving raw earth and exposed roots, something is working -- dozens of small, wet, vaguely humanoid shapes made of river-mud and clay, moving with the busy purposefulness of ants. They are packing mud into the wound, weaving roots through the fill, and smoothing the surface with hands that are more suggestion than anatomy. One of them notices you watching. It stops, tilts its featureless head, and extends a hand tha...

3.name: Nak
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- brackish; the murky, salt-tinged water where river meets sea, neither fully fresh nor fully salt
3.environment: Pekakarlik Docks (the timber-pylon dock-complexes of Pekakarlik coastal and estuarine trading-posts -- the pilings, jetties, and harbour structures where river-barges meet sea-going vessels along the Apelian coast)
3.description: The dock is old Pekakarlik construction -- heavy timber pylons driven into the estuary-mud, planked with salt-treated oak, worn smooth by a century of barge-keels and bare feet. At the dock's end, where the pylons meet the water, something lives between the pilings. The children see it first -- they always do -- a crocodilian shape the size of a large dog, long-snouted and dark-scaled, watching the dock-traffic from the shadows between the timbers with bright, unblinking eyes. It is not a crocod...

3.name: Noppera-Bō
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; a creature that registers on Kin-sense as a blank space in the shape of a person, like a silhouette cut from the world
3.environment: Great Forests (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth forest -- particularly the twilight-zones between Tanuki settlements and the deeper forest, where the lantern-light ends and the shadows begin)
3.description: The path through the Tanuki grove is well-lit -- paper lanterns strung between the trees, their warm light turning the forest-floor into a patchwork of gold and shadow. You have been walking for twenty minutes and you are beginning to suspect that the path is longer than it should be. The figure ahead of you on the path is a Tanuki woman -- round-faced, small, carrying a basket of mushrooms. She has been walking ahead of you since you entered the grove. She has not turned around. You call out. ...

3.name: Púca
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- crossroads; the resonance of the place where road meets road, where earth and air mix at the boundary between tame and wild, cultivated and untamed, known and unknown
3.environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the crossroads, forest-edges, river-fords, and boundary-points where the Great Forests meet the Eternal Steppe -- specifically the transitional zones where no single Kin culture claims authority and where the rules of civilisation begin to fray)
3.description: The horse is standing at the crossroads where the forest meets the steppe, and it is the most beautiful horse you have ever seen -- midnight-black, tall, with eyes that gleam like copper coins in the last light of day. It is watching you with an intelligence that horses do not possess, and its ears are pricked forward with an expression you would describe as amused. The Pasua ranger beside you says, very quietly, 'Don't get on.' You were not planning to get on. But now that she's said it, you ...

3.name: Unicorn
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- forest-heart; the resonance of the deepest, purest forest-earth -- soil that has never been ploughed, turned, or contaminated, the Lord of Stone's domain expressed as the foundation of untouched wilderness
3.environment: Great Forests (the deepest, most pristine zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the old-growth interiors where Kin settlements have never reached, where the forest-floor has been undisturbed for millennia, and where the elemental purity of the earth is absolute)
3.description: The forest clearing is lit from within -- not by sunlight (the canopy is too thick) but by something else, a quality of light that has no visible source and no shadow. The creature standing in the clearing is a horse -- or it looks like a horse, the way a cathedral looks like a building. It is white, impossibly white, the white of fresh snow before anyone has walked on it, and its mane falls like water. The horn is the colour of old ivory, spiralling from the centre of its forehead with the mat...

3.name: Water Mephit
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- mist-mischief; the Lady's domain at its most trivial and annoying
3.environment: Great River (river-banks, bridge-hollows, canal-sides, and anywhere the Great River generates mist)
3.description: The river-mist thickens -- unnaturally, in a column that resolves into a grinning, dripping, knee-high figure with wings like wet sails and a face that is all malicious delight. It spits a stream of acid-tinged water at your boot and cackles.

1.name: Air Elemental, Small
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Wind Lord (Air)
1.environment: Any windy locale; most common in the Eternal Steppe (storm-paths, open grasslands), Giant's Teeth (high passes, summit winds), and Twin Rivers (Great Forest canopy during storms)
1.description: The wind gathers where no wind should be. Dust rises in a spiral, leaves whip into a column, and then it is there -- a shape made of rushing air, its outline barely visible except where debris traces its form. It smells of high places: ozone, cold rain, the thin air above the mountaintops. It does not speak, but the sound it makes as it moves is like breathing drawn through a cathedral.

1.name: Domovoi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fire dominant; the domovoi is a hearth-spirit, born where fire meets stone
1.environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki dwarf forges, hearthstones, and workshops -- always inside a structure with an active fire source); rare secondary presence in Eruskan river-settlements and Pekakarlik oasis-forges
1.description: The forge-fire is banked for the night, the embers glowing low, and something moves in the warm space between the anvil and the hearthstone. It is small -- no taller than a dwarf-child -- and shaped like a very old dwarf who has been compressed by time until everything non-essential has been squeezed out, leaving only beard, eyes, and an expression of severe domestic judgment. It checks the fire. It checks the tools. It picks up a hammer that has been left on the wrong hook and places it on the...

1.name: Grig
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the grig is the playful overlap of earth and air; the cricket in the grass, the music on the breeze
1.environment: Great Forests (forest clearings, meadow-margins, stream-banks, and the sun-dappled edges of the canopy -- wherever the forest is light and warm enough for music)
1.description: You hear the fiddle first. Something is playing a fiddle in the undergrowth -- playing it well, playing it fast, playing a reel that makes your feet want to move before your brain can object. You part the ferns and see the musician: a creature the size of your hand, cricket-legged, fairy-winged, grinning like a lunatic, sawing away at a fiddle the size of a matchstick with a bow the size of a needle. It sees you seeing it and plays faster.

1.name: Mazzikin
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mischievous; the air that steals your hat, the wind that snuffs your candle
1.environment: Any (mazzikin are found wherever goblins gather -- goblin camps, trading-posts, and caravans in the Deepwater Marches, but also goblin-Kin border-communities, Eruskan market-towns with goblin populations, and isolated settlements where the boundary between fey and mortal is thin)
1.description: You cannot see them. That is the point -- mazzikin are invisible, bodiless, and everywhere, and the only evidence of their presence is the mischief. Your bootlaces are tied together. Your waterskin is empty though you filled it an hour ago. The goblin trader's pack has been rearranged so that the heavy items are on top and the fragile items are on the bottom. From somewhere near the ceiling, something giggles -- a high, musical laugh that sounds like a child who has never been told no.

1.name: Nixie
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- gentle; the clear water of shallow lakes, sunlight on the surface, the warmth of still pools
1.environment: Rivers and Lakes (freshwater lakes, slow rivers, mill-ponds, and spring-fed pools throughout the Great River system and the Deepwater Marches -- anywhere that still, clear freshwater collects in sufficient depth)
1.description: The lake is clear and shallow at the edges, the kind of place where children wade and fishermen cast from the bank. You see the light first -- a flicker of green-gold beneath the surface, like sunlight catching a fish-scale, except the light moves with purpose. A head breaks the surface: small, angular, green-skinned, with large dark eyes and hair the colour of waterweed. She treads water effortlessly and regards you with the frank curiosity of something that owns this lake and considers you a...

1.name: Water Elemental, Small
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water)
1.environment: Any body of water; most common in the Great River and its tributaries, the Kailian and Apelian Seas, and the Deepwater Marches
1.description: The river rises. Not floods -- the water level does not change -- but a column of river-water pulls itself upright, shaping limbs and a torso and something that might be a head from the current itself. It stands on the river's surface like a person stands on a floor, and within its translucent body you can see river-debris turning slowly: pebbles, weeds, a small fish swimming in confused circles. When it moves, the sound is rain on stone.

4.name: Aranea
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, with Air dominant
4.environment: Twin Rivers and Kinsweave (Great Forests, deep canopy; especially common in the Hasura heartlands and the old-growth Deepwood border)
4.description: It looks like a spider -- a hand-sized spider, mottled brown and grey, building a web between two branches with the unhurried patience of a craftsman who has all day. Then you notice the web's pattern: not random, not instinctive, but geometric -- a mandala of concentric shapes that no spider should know how to make. And then it turns, and the cluster of eyes that looks back at you has something behind them that spiders' eyes do not have.

4.name: Bakeneko
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dual affinity, Air dominant
4.environment: Twin Rivers (Great Forests, Tanuki groves and longhouse settlements; occasionally in Hasura border-villages where forest meets cultivated land)
4.description: The cat sits on the rooftop of a Tanuki longhouse, washing its face with one paw. It has been sitting there since morning. It was sitting there yesterday, too. The Tanuki children wave to it; the Tanuki elders do not. They have noticed that the cat's shadow does not match its posture, and that its tail -- if you watch long enough -- splits at the tip and then mends itself when it realises you are looking.

4.name: Hakuturi
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- the living forest; root-water and dark soil, growth expressed as patience
4.environment: Great Forests (the old-growth stands of the Kovaka territories -- the southern and island-fringe forests where ancient trees have stood for millennia undisturbed; never found in young growth, managed forest, or areas cleared within living memory)
4.description: The tree is old. Older than the Kovaka settlement at its base, older than the trail that leads to it, older than anything human memory can account for. You came to cut a branch -- a single branch, for a canoe-rib -- and now you cannot lift the axe. Not because something is stopping you. Because the tree is looking at you. The bark has rearranged itself into a face that was always there and always watching, and the face is not angry. It is patient. It is waiting for you to understand why you sho...

4.name: Huldra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the forest-river border; where water meets earth and both are alive
4.environment: Forest-River Borders (the banks and shallows of rivers that flow through the Great Forests -- specifically the transition zone where forest canopy overhangs running water, and the worlds of wood and water overlap)
4.description: She is standing in the river. The water is waist-deep and moving fast, and she is standing in it as though it were a warm bath -- barefoot on the stones, her back to you, her long hair trailing in the current. She is beautiful. You can tell that even from behind, from the line of her shoulders and the way the water parts around her as though it likes her. Then she turns, and you see her face, and she is more beautiful than you expected. And then you see her back -- or rather, you see that her b...

4.name: Hydra
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- hydras are creatures of the deep marsh, saturated with stagnant water-energy
4.environment: Marshes (the Tidebloom Reach, the Deepwater Marches wetlands, and the marshy lowlands where the Great River spreads into seasonal flood-plains -- any warm, wet, low-lying terrain with sufficient prey)
4.description: Five heads. Five necks. One body. The body is the size of a horse and scaled in dark, wet green; the necks are each ten feet long and independently mobile, weaving and striking like snakes operated by a single furious brain. When one head lunges, the others cover it. When one head is hurt, the others strike at the attacker. The creature does not think -- five heads do not produce five times the intelligence, they produce five times the aggression.

4.name: Otyugh
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberrant; otyughs register on Kin-sense as a faint, unpleasant signal that doesn't map to any elemental framework
4.environment: City Sewers and Dungeons (the sewage-systems, refuse-pits, and underground waste-channels of Eruskan cities, Vestri mining-camps, and any settlement large enough to generate concentrated organic waste)
4.description: The sewer smells exactly as bad as you expected, but the thing in the sewer smells worse. It occupies the junction where three tunnels meet -- a bulbous, three-legged mass of mottled brown-grey flesh, waist-deep in the effluent, with two long tentacles rising from its body like the antennae of something that evolved specifically to live in places no other creature would tolerate. Its third appendage -- a thick, eyestalk on a muscular neck -- swivels toward you with the calm, unsurprised attentio...

4.name: River Serpent
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- warden; the resonance of the living river, water that is healthy and watched and protected, the Lady of the Sea's freshwater domain maintained through her eldest servants
4.environment: Great River (the full length of the Great River and its major tributaries -- from the headwaters in the Giant's Teeth foothills through the Eruskan river-cities to the delta where the Great River meets the Apelian Sea; also present in the Twin Rivers region and Kinsweave lake systems)
4.description: The Great River is deep here -- deep enough that you cannot see the bottom from the barge's railing, deep enough that the Pekakarlik boatman navigates by current-reading and memory rather than by sight. And then, in the deep green below, something moves. It is long -- longer than the barge -- and it moves with the effortless sinuosity of a creature for which the river is not habitat but home in the most fundamental sense. You catch a flash of iridescent scale, blue-green and silver, and a head t...

4.name: Shedim
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- liminal; shedim exist in the twilight between elemental signatures, in the perceptual gaps where Kin-sense transitions between readings and cannot resolve clearly
4.environment: Twilight Zones (the liminal spaces at the edges of goblin settlements -- the grey areas between day and night, between forest and clearing, between the wild static of goblin communities and the blank absence of uninhabited wilderness; shedim manifest where the world is between states)
4.description: The twilight is wrong. You have walked through twilight a hundred times -- the grey hour between day and night when the world is neither lit nor dark, when shadows have no source and distance becomes unreliable. This twilight does not end. It has been grey for twenty minutes, and the sun is not setting and has not risen, and the goblin settlement ahead is quiet in a way that goblin settlements are never quiet. Something stands in the gap between the last house and the treeline -- a shape that i...

6.name: Bunyip
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water)
6.environment: Apelian Sea (coastal shallows, river-mouths, tidal pools, and sheltered coves along the Deepwater Marches coastline); secondary presence at the Sea Falls and in the brackish estuaries where the Great River meets the Apelian basin
6.description: The water at the edge of the sea-lake goes still -- not the stillness of calm water, but the stillness of water holding its breath. Then a shape surfaces: seal-dark, heavy-shouldered, with a head that seems to shift between dog and crocodile depending on the angle. Its mouth opens and it produces a sound -- not a roar, not a bark, but something between the two, a bellowing cry that reverberates off the cliff face and turns the blood in your arms to ice water. The fisherfolk are already running....

6.name: Culsans
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dual affinity; the culsans embodies the meeting of currents, the point where rivers converge and winds cross
6.environment: Great River (river-forks, confluence points, canal junctions, and crossroads along the Great River and its major tributaries -- always at a point where two paths, two currents, or two directions meet); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-forks in the Twin Rivers region
6.description: The gate stands at the river-fork where the Twin Rivers meet -- two pillars of Giant-cut stone, the carvings worn smooth by centuries of water and wind. Between the pillars, a figure stands facing both directions at once. It has two faces -- one looking upstream, one looking downstream -- set on a single head that rotates slowly, continuously, like the hands of a clock that measures something other than time. Its body is carved from the same stone as the gate, or perhaps the gate was carved from...

6.name: Girallon
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the canopy and the trunk; girallons are creatures of the vertical forest
6.environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests -- specifically the mid-to-upper canopy zone, 60-120 feet above the forest floor, where the branches are thick enough to support their weight and the undergrowth is too dense for ground-travel)
6.description: Four arms. That is what you notice first -- not the size (though it is massive, nine feet at the shoulder), not the white fur (though it is matted and streaked with old blood), not the fangs (though they are longer than your fingers). Four arms, each one thick as a man's thigh, each one ending in a hand that could crush a helmet like an egg. The creature drops from the canopy on four of those arms and lands in front of you on two legs, and the remaining four spread wide, and every hand is open...

6.name: Jorogumo
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- aberration; breach resonance, deliberately masked
6.environment: Gatefall Reach (the waterfall-basins, pool-edges, and cliff-hollows of the Gatefall -- the great chain of waterfalls in Hasura territory where the river-systems descend from the highland forests to the lowland valleys; specifically the remote, infrequently-patrolled falls where prey can be taken without immediate discovery)
6.description: She is beautiful. She is always beautiful -- that is the point, that is the design, that is the trap. She sits at the edge of the Gatefall, where the forest canopy overhangs the waterfall-pool, and she combs her hair with her fingers and watches the water and waits for someone to find her. The Hasura scouts found the cocoons first -- three of them, wrapped in silk the colour of moonlight, hanging from the underside of the cliff-ledge behind the falls. Inside each cocoon, a Hasura elf. Two were ...

6.name: Maero (Brute)
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- artificial earth-attunement, cosmetic only
6.environment: Mountain Valleys (the densely-forested mountain valleys of Kovaka coastal territory -- the steep-walled river-valleys, old-growth ravines, and fog-bound coastal forests of the southwestern Great Forests)
6.organization: Solitary, pair, or band (3-5, rare -- degraded War-era formation)
6.description: The Kovaka call them te tangata mohoao -- the wild people -- but there is nothing of a person in what steps out of the tree-line. It is eight feet of matted hair and raw-boned muscle, its arms too long for its body, its hands thick-fingered and blunt as mallets. The face is wrong in the particular way that all Brute faces are wrong: close enough to Kin to trigger recognition, far enough from Kin to trigger revulsion. It smells of rotting bark and swamp-water and something chemical beneath both ...

6.name: Oni
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- dark, primal; deep-forest earth, the resonance of soil that has been dark since the world began, where the forest-floor is older than the trees growing on it
6.environment: Deep Forests (Hasura territory and beyond -- the oldest, densest interior of the Great Forests where the canopy blocks all direct sunlight and the undergrowth is primordial)
6.description: The deep forest changes character past the Hasura border-markers. The trees are older, the canopy is lower, and the undergrowth is thick enough to hide a horse. The Hasura rangers who guided you this far have stopped. They will not go further. They point to a mark on a tree -- a claw-mark at nine feet, old and deep -- and speak the name quietly, as though the creature might hear: oni. In the forest beyond the marker, something laughs. It is a deep laugh, rich with amusement, and it comes from t...

6.name: Popobawa
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- shadow outsider; the popobawa exists in the space between elemental resonances, a predator of the gap, registering on Kin-sense as concentrated dread -- not absence (like Dómnathar void) but active malice, the feeling of being watched by something that feeds on fear
6.environment: Scorchspire Settlements (the stone-built villages, farming-terraces, and valley-floor communities of Taraf-Imro territory in the Scorchspire foothills -- specifically the isolated settlements at volcanic-valley edges where darkness is absolute at night and where the gap between houses creates shadows deep enough for a predator to hide)
6.organization: Solitary (always -- popobawa are aggressively solitary and territorial)
6.description: The settlement goes quiet after dark -- not the comfortable quiet of a village at rest, but the held-breath quiet of prey that knows the predator is already inside the pen. The Taraf-Imro warrior on the roof has not moved in twenty minutes. He is watching the shadows between the stone houses, watching for the thing that does not have a fixed shape, that moves through walls without opening doors, that smells of sulphur and bat-musk and something worse -- the sweet-rot stink of a creature that fe...

6.name: Tendriculos
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ravenous growth; the resonance of earth-fertility pushed to destructive excess, soil so rich and so hungry that everything planted in it grows beyond control and consumes everything around it
6.environment: Deepwood (the densest, darkest zones of the Great Forests -- the old-growth interiors where the canopy blocks nearly all light and the undergrowth is so thick that navigation is by sound and touch rather than sight)
6.description: The Deepwood is dense -- dense enough that sunlight reaches the forest floor only in thin, scattered beams, and the undergrowth is a tangle of moss, root, and fallen branch. The mound ahead looks like a compost-heap, green with new growth. It is not a compost-heap. The vines that compose it are moving, slowly, independently, and the opening at the mound's centre -- the one that looks like a hollow in the vegetation -- is lined with thorns the length of daggers, and it is a mouth.

6.name: Tipua
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Lady of the Sea (Earth/Water) -- landmark spirit; the resonance of specific geographical features that have persisted long enough to develop awareness -- a boulder, a spring, a tree, a cliff-face -- the elemental system acknowledging places that matter
6.environment: Kovaka Landmarks (the named geographical features of Kovaka coastal-forest territory -- ancient trees, distinctive rock-formations, hot springs, river-forks, and cliff-edges that have served as navigational reference-points and cultural markers for centuries)
6.organization: Solitary (always bound to a specific landmark)
6.description: The landmark has always been here -- the split boulder at the river-fork, the ancient pohutukawa tree at the cliff-edge, the hot spring where the valley narrows. The Kovaka have names for each one, and the names are older than the settlements. Today the landmark is different. The split boulder has shifted -- not moved, but the gap between the halves is wider, and in the gap stands a woman who was not there a moment ago. She is beautiful, dark-haired, barefoot, and her eyes are the colour of the...

6.name: Tuchulcha
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- drowned malice; the resonance of water in places where it should not be -- flooded cellars, submerged streets, drowned foundations -- the Lady of the Sea's domain claiming territory that was built for air, converting civilisation to ruin through patient inundation
6.environment: Drowned Places (the flood-damaged, water-logged, and permanently submerged structures of Eruskan river-cities -- specifically the cellars, undercrofts, and lower storeys of buildings that have been partially or fully claimed by the Great River's seasonal floods and never reclaimed by the living)
6.description: The drowned cellar is knee-deep in black water -- river-water that has seeped through the foundation of the Eruskan merchant-house that has been abandoned since the flood. The water is still. The smell is mould and old wood and something underneath: sulphur, faint and sharp. The torch-light catches the surface of the water and the surface catches something back -- a face, reflected from below, that is not your face. It has three faces, actually: one human, one serpent, one vulture, arranged on ...

6.name: Uthikoloshe
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- shadow-current; the resonance of water in shadow, river-current that flows through the darkest places, the Lady of the Sea's domain where her light does not reach -- not corrupted water (like a spirit naga's domain) but shadowed water, water that has become a medium for darkness
special_qualities: vulnerability to sunlight on dry land
6.environment: Warg Territory (the rivers, streams, and water-crossings of Warg pack territory on the Eternal Steppe -- specifically the fords, shallow crossings, and river-bends where Warg packs cross between seasonal grazing-grounds, where the water is accessible and the uthikoloshe can ambush wading creatures)
6.description: The river-crossing is shallow -- knee-deep, cold, and fast-moving. The Warg pack-scout wades in without hesitation and stops. The water around his ankles has darkened -- not with mud but with something else, a shadow that has no source and no shape, a presence in the water that is cold in a way that river-water in summer should not be. The scout backs out slowly. 'Uthikoloshe,' he says to the pack-leader. His voice is flat. 'In the water. We go around.' The pack-leader looks at the river -- the ...

6.name: Wyvern
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother / Lady of Fire (Air/Fire) -- warm-hill predator; the resonance of warm updraughts rising from sun-heated hillsides, the air-fire intersection that produces the thermal columns wyverns ride to altitude -- not the intellectual fire of dragons or the sacred wind of thunderbirds, but the simple, functional heat that a predator uses to fly
6.environment: Warm Hills (the sun-heated hill-country and low ridgelines of central and southern Oreka -- specifically the foothills where thermal updraughts are reliable, where the terrain provides cliff-nesting sites and the plains below provide prey, and where the warm-season climate supports year-round flying)
6.description: The shape in the sky resolves as it banks -- two legs, two wings, a barbed tail longer than its body, and a head that is all jaw and no subtlety. It is not a dragon. It does not have a dragon's intelligence, a dragon's breath, or a dragon's treasure-hoard ambition. What it has is a tail-stinger loaded with enough venom to drop a horse, a wingspan that lets it carry that horse into the sky, and the single-minded predatory focus of a creature that has identified you as the right size for dinner.

5.name: Al Karisi
flags: elemental_affinity:Distorted -- the Al Karisi retains a corrupted echo of whatever element it was attuned to in life, but the signature is broken and painful to read
5.environment: Any settled region where Kin give birth -- not bound to a specific territory but drawn to the act of birth itself. Most commonly reported in the Kinsweave border-kingdoms, Tidebloom Reach, and Eruskan river-settlements where births occur in isolated conditions
5.description: The room smells of blood and birth and then, without warning, of something else -- something dry and bitter, like ash mixed with old flowers. The new mother is sleeping, the child beside her. A shape stands in the corner that was not there a moment ago: grey, translucent, wearing the face of a woman who looks almost -- but not quite -- like someone the midwife once knew. Its hands are long. Its nails are longer. It drifts toward the cradle with the slow, purposeful focus of something that has co...

5.name: Cù Sìth
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the border between wind and root, the place where forest meets grassland
5.environment: Forest-Steppe Border (the transitional zone where the Great Forests thin into grassland -- specifically the Pasua-claimed stretches of the western and southern forest-edge, the Kinsweave-Steppe frontier, and the wooded margins of the Twin Rivers region)
5.description: The dog is too large. That is the first thing -- it stands shoulder-height to a horse, its body lean and deep-chested, built for endurance rather than speed. Its fur is dark green, the colour of moss on old stone, and it moves through the undergrowth without disturbing a single branch. Its eyes are gold and ancient and watching you with an intelligence that no dog should possess. It does not bark. It does not growl. It simply appears at the tree-line, considers you, and waits to see what you w...

5.name: Green Hag
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted; the green hag is the Lady's domain gone stagnant and poisonous
5.environment: Tidebloom Marshes (the tidal marshlands of the Tidebloom Reach, particularly the deep-marsh zones where freshwater rivers meet saltwater tidal intrusions and the water is neither one thing nor the other)
5.organization: Solitary or covey (3 hags of any kind plus other creatures)
5.description: She lives where the water goes bad. Not where the water is dangerous -- that is different, honest danger, the kind you can see. She lives where the water is wrong: where the marsh-pools turn the wrong colour, where the fish float belly-up without visible cause, where the air tastes of sweetness that your body tells you means rot. She is there, somewhere in the murk, watching you with yellow eyes that she has not had to disguise in decades because no one comes here anymore.

5.name: Kinnari
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Youngest Brother (Air) -- the kinnari is the waterfall-song, the place where water and air create music
5.environment: Great River and Sea Falls (the major waterfalls of Oreka -- the Sea Falls along the Pekakarlik coast, the Grand Falls on the Great River, and the cascading river-systems of the Giant's Teeth; always at waterfalls, never in still water or open sky)
5.description: The music comes from the waterfall's mist. Not from behind the waterfall, not from above it -- from the mist itself, from the place where falling water becomes air. The voice is singing, and the voice is the most beautiful thing you have ever heard. It is a woman's voice, high and clear and resonant in a way that no mortal throat produces, and when the mist parts, you see her: bird-legged, bird-winged, woman-bodied, hovering in the spray-column with effortless grace, her feathers iridescent wi...

5.name: Kitsune
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- mutable; the kitsune's air-resonance shifts and changes like the wind, never quite the same twice, reflecting the fey's shapeshifting nature
5.environment: Great Forests (the deciduous and mixed forests of Hasura territory -- forest-edges, shrine-clearings, ancient game-trails, and the borderlands where managed forest meets wild wood)
5.organization: Solitary or skulk (2-4, always related -- a parent and offspring, or siblings)
5.description: The fox was there a moment ago. You saw it -- a red fox, large, sitting at the tree-line with its tail curled around its feet, watching you with an intelligence that foxes should not have. You blinked. Now it is a woman -- or was a woman, or is becoming a woman, or is something in between that your brain is trying to resolve into one shape or the other and failing. She smiles at you with the fox's eyes in a woman's face, and you understand that the shape was never the point.

5.name: Näck
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- dark; the pull of deep water, the current beneath the surface that drags you under while the surface stays calm
5.environment: Sea Falls (the great coastal waterfalls along the Pekakarlik sea-fall trade-route -- the plunge-pools, mist-caves, and spray-hollows where freshwater meets the Apelian; secondary presence at isolated inland waterfalls in the Great River system, always at falls with deep plunge-pools)
5.description: The Sea Falls roar, and beneath the roar there is music. Not the kinnari's waterfall-song -- you have heard that, and it was beautiful in a way that healed. This is beautiful in a way that takes. A violin, played from behind the curtain of falling water by hands you cannot see, producing a melody so perfect it hollows you out. Your feet are wet. You have walked into the pool at the falls' base without deciding to. The water is at your knees. The music is louder. The player steps through the sp...

5.name: Phi Tai Hong
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- violent death imprint; a phi tai hong is the psychic scar of a violent or unjust death burned into a location, grief and rage so concentrated it persists after the body is gone
5.alignment: Always chaotic evil (the original person's alignment is irrelevant -- the phi tai hong is pure concentrated anguish, not a continuation of the deceased's personality)
5.environment: Great River Gorge (the narrow cliff-walled sections of the Great River where the water cuts through ancient stone -- particularly the deep gorges between Pekakarlik river-towns where the current is swift, the walls are high, and deaths by drowning, falling, or ambush are most common)
5.organization: Solitary (always -- each phi tai hong is a unique death)
5.description: The gorge narrows where the Great River cuts through the old rock, and in the narrowing the air changes. Not temperature -- something else. The Pekakarlik boatman has gone quiet. His pole rests against the gunwale, untouched, and the barge drifts on the current alone. He is watching the cliff-face, specifically the ledge thirty feet above the waterline where the wildflowers grow too thick and too bright for a place that gets no sunlight. 'Someone died there,' he says. 'Badly.' You look again. ...

5.name: Spider Eater
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-predator; the resonance of the Great Forest's upper airspace, wind filtered through leaves and channelled between tree-trunks
5.environment: Great Forests (the mid-canopy and upper-canopy zones of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where giant spider populations are dense enough to support spider eater breeding cycles)
5.description: The thing drops from the canopy -- thirty feet of membranous wing spanning between a body like a wasp's, a head like a horse's, and a stinger the length of your forearm. It is not falling. It is diving, and the target is not you but the giant spider in the web between the oaks. The stinger drives into the spider's abdomen with surgical precision, deposits something, and the creature is airborne again before the spider can react. In three days, the spider's body will burst open. The larva insid...

5.name: Vanth
flags: elemental_affinity:All four Elemental Lords -- grave-stillness; the resonance of all four elements settling into equilibrium, the moment where fire cools, water stills, wind falls silent, and earth receives -- not the harmonious balance of the qilin or the learned wisdom of the simurgh, but the quiet cessation that follows the end of a life, when the elements that comprised a living being return to the world
5.environment: Battlefields (the sites of mass Kin death -- battlefields, massacre-sites, plague-graves, and disaster-zones, specifically within the first days or weeks after death, when the recently dead have not yet been fully committed to the earth and the boundary between life and death is thinnest; most commonly documented at Eruskan military engagements)
5.description: The battlefield is three days old. The crows have come and gone. The burial-detail has done what it can. And yet something remains -- a figure standing at the field's edge where the dead are thickest, motionless, masked, carrying a staff that is neither weapon nor walking-stick but something between. It is the height of a tall man but too thin, wrapped in a cloak the colour of old bronze, and its face is a mask -- featureless, smooth, with no eyes and no mouth but a surface that somehow conveys...

2.name: Changeling
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- fey affinity aligned with the forest (Wind through branches, Earth in roots)
2.environment: Great Forests (deep forest, near Kin settlements at the forest edge; also found in Twin Rivers forest-villages and the southern Kinsweave where forest extends into the plains)
2.organization: Solitary (always when placed in a Kin household; a small clutch of 2-5 may exist in the deep forest in natural form before dispersal)
2.description: The child in the cradle is wrong. The parents know it. The neighbours know it. The child itself -- if it is a child -- knows it, and watches the household with eyes that are too old and too amused for its face. It is the right size, the right shape, and it eats when fed, cries when wet, and sleeps when held. But it is not the child that was there yesterday. Something in the forest took the real one and left this in its place.

2.name: Hippogriff
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- similar to griffons but lighter, more air-dominant; the hippogriff is wind-over-hill rather than wind-over-mountain
2.environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (the low, grassy hills and upland meadows of the Giant's Teeth margins -- lower elevation than griffon territory, in terrain where grassland meets slope and thermal-updrafts are strong)
2.description: The horse has wings. That is the simplest, most reductive description, and it is wrong -- the creature is not a horse with wings any more than a griffon is an eagle with a lion's body. It is its own thing: part raptor, part equine, shaped by the same elemental forces that produced griffons but with a different emphasis. Where the griffon is all predator, the hippogriff is half-wild, half-willing, and that willingness is what makes it valuable.

2.name: Satyr
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) -- forest-edge; the resonance of where cultivated land meets wildwood, earth-under-leaves meeting wind-through-branches, the boundary between the tamed and the untamed expressed as music
2.environment: Great Forests (the mid-forest zones between deep canopy and forest-edge -- specifically the clearings, stream-banks, and meadow-openings where sunlight reaches the ground and the forest is at its most welcoming and most deceptive)
2.description: The music reaches you before the musician does. It is coming from deep in the forest -- a pipe-melody that winds between the trees like smoke, changing key with each turn of the path, and you find yourself walking toward it without having decided to. The undergrowth parts. The clearing opens. He is sitting on a fallen oak, legs crossed -- the legs of a goat, furred and hooved, below the bare-chested torso of a man who looks like he has never done anything more strenuous than smile. He is playin...

7.name: Charun
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- dual affinity; stone shaped by heat, the forge-gate, the threshold between states
7.environment: Great River (river-gates, bridge-arches, canal locks, and portals along the Great River and its major tributaries -- always at a point of transition, where water passes from one state or region to another); most commonly encountered at Eruskan-maintained river-gates
7.description: The river barge drifts toward the stone gate where the Great River narrows between two carved pillars -- remnants of Giant architecture, now maintained by Eruskan hands. Standing on the west bank is a figure that should not be there: tall, heavy-framed, grey-skinned, with a face like weathered stone and eyes like embers in a dead fire. It carries a hammer. Not a weapon -- or not only a weapon. A tool. The kind of tool used to shape stone, break locks, and open things that were meant to stay clo...

7.name: Dire Bear
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth (the mountain-and-forest bear, stone-dense and rooted)
7.environment: Giant's Teeth Foothills (forested mountain slopes, river valleys in the lower Giant's Teeth, the wooded approaches to Orean plateau settlements)

7.name: Skinwalker
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone / Youngest Brother (Earth/Air) -- stolen, shifting; a skinwalker's resonance is not its own -- it is a corrupted fragment of whatever creature's form it has most recently worn, earth and air twisted into something that mimics life without possessing it
7.environment: Eternal Steppe (the open grasslands, river-valleys, and camp-grounds of Mytroan territory -- specifically the edges of Mytroan settlements and herding-routes, where the steppe meets the dark and the boundary between human space and wild space is thinnest)
7.description: The Mytroan camp-fire is burning low, and the herder on watch is the last one awake. The horse at the picket-line is nervous -- stamping, pulling at its tether, nostrils flared. The herder watches the horse and does not watch the darkness behind him, and that is the mistake. Something comes out of the steppe on four legs -- a wolf, large and dark, moving with a wolf's easy lope -- and then it stops, and the wolf unfolds. The hind-legs straighten. The forelegs become arms. The spine elongates. Th...

7.name: Spectre
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- extinguished; the resonance of fire that has gone out, heat that has been removed, the Lady of Fire's domain inverted into its opposite -- not cold (which is the absence of heat) but the memory of heat, the agonising awareness of warmth that no longer exists
7.environment: Ashen Hollows (the dead volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills -- places where volcanic activity ceased, where the Lady of Fire's presence once burned and now does not, leaving a residue of elemental grief that produces the most powerful incorporeal undead in the region)
7.description: The Ashen Hollows are the places where fire died -- the volcanic valleys of the Scorchspire foothills where eruptions once poured lava and now pour nothing, where the ground is cold and grey and the trees are petrified stumps. Nothing lives here. That is the first sign that something dead does. The shape that rises from the grey earth is not smoke -- it is darker than smoke, more defined, and it moves with the deliberate purpose of something that remembers being alive and hates you for still be...

7.name: Tengu
flags: elemental_affinity:Youngest Brother (Air) -- canopy-wind; the resonance of wind in the highest forest, air channelled through branch-architecture into currents that only canopy-dwellers know, the Youngest Brother's domain at its most refined and most playful
7.environment: High Canopy (the upper canopy of the Great Forests -- specifically the Hasura canopy-settlement zones, where the forest-architecture of branch-platforms and vine-bridges creates an aerial environment that tengu navigate with natural ease)
7.description: The Hasura canopy-platform is a hundred feet above the forest floor, and the creature standing at the edge of the platform should not be able to stand there -- the branch is too narrow for anything larger than a squirrel. But the tengu stands with the casual balance of something born to the air, its taloned feet gripping the bark with an ease that makes the Hasura sentinel beside you look clumsy by comparison. It is man-shaped -- roughly -- with a raven's head, a raven's beak, and wings folded a...

7.name: Vodyanoy
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- river-lord; the resonance of a specific stretch of river claimed by an intelligence that manages it -- not corrupted water (like a spirit naga's domain) and not shadowed water (like an uthikoloshe's crossing) but governed water, river that has a master who keeps it healthy, productive, and under absolute personal control
7.environment: Great River / Kailian Deep (the deep pools, bridge-hollows, and river-bends of the Great River and its major tributaries, and the freshwater deeps of the Kailian Sea coast -- specifically the pools beneath bridges, dams, and river-structures where the water is deep enough and permanent enough to sustain a vodyanoy's territorial claim)
7.organization: Solitary or court (1 vodyanoy plus 2-4 water fey attendants -- lasa, water mephits, or small water elementals)
7.description: The river-pool beneath the bridge is deep -- deeper than the river should be at this point, the bottom invisible even in clear water. Something lives in the depth, and the Eruskan rivermen know what it is because the bridge has been here for four hundred years and the thing below the bridge has been here longer. It surfaces at dusk. It is man-shaped -- roughly -- with a face like a catfish given human proportions: wide-mouthed, bearded with trailing whisker-tendrils, and green-skinned, covered i...

7.name: Water Naga
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- guardian; unlike the spirit naga's corrupted-water, a water naga's resonance is protective -- river-current that watches, water that guards
7.environment: Great River Pools (the deep, still pools along the Great River's middle stretch -- specifically the naturally occurring pools where the river slows and deepens, creating habitats that water nagas claim as territories and guard with territorial but not malicious intent)
7.description: The pool-water is clear -- deceptively clear, the kind of clarity that means nothing has been disturbing the bottom for a long time. The naga is coiled on the submerged ledge like a crown of emerald-green scales, its humanoid face composed, watchful, and radiating an intelligence that is not hostile but is absolutely, unmistakably evaluating you. It is deciding whether you are a threat, a resource, or an interesting conversation.

7.name: Silentborn Assassin (Rogue 5 / Assassin 2)
flags: silentborn
hostile
kin_silent
boss
special_attacks: Death Attack (DC 15, paralyze or kill on 3-round study)
special_qualities: Kin-Sense Silence (Ex): undetectable by Kin-sense; registers as no-signature at all, as if dead. DC 25 Will save or relevant class-level check to overcome.
7.environment: Tidebloom Reach (Ashen Hollows raider camps, hidden Dómnathar refugee enclaves near the Tomb of Kings); forest; Twin Rivers infiltration cells; any urban Dómnathar operation
7.description: A killer the Spur Tower sends when subtlety is essential. He has been knifed, hanged, and drowned by three different Kin city-watches and all three reported him dead and buried. He was not.

7.name: Half-Dómnathar Broker (Bard 5 / Rogue 2)
flags: half_domnathar
hostile
boss
special_qualities: Faintly luminous eyes in low light
7.environment: Twin Rivers (Gorge-Bridge infiltrator cells, Unstrung Philosophers' hidden refuges in the Riverwind Heights); Deepwater Ashcrown Warren as an embedded broker
7.description: A handsome, neatly-dressed figure with slightly elongated ears who passes for human at any social distance. She has a perfect smile and a perfect memory for names.

9.name: Dragon Turtle
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lady of Fire (Fire) -- dual affinity; the dragon turtle is the Lady of the Sea's domain armoured in the Lady of Fire's heat
9.environment: Kailian Sea (deep channels, submerged seamounts, and the warmer currents of the eastern Kailian basin); secondary presence in the deep Apelian Sea (rare) and at the Great River's mouth where freshwater meets salt
9.description: The sea ahead goes flat. Not calm -- flat, as though the water itself has been pressed down by something enormous beneath the surface. Then the shell rises: a ridge of stone and coral the size of a small island, streaming water, trailing kelp gardens that have been growing on its back for decades. The head emerges last -- a dragon's head on a turtle's neck, armoured in scales the colour of deep ocean, with eyes like amber lanterns. The mouth opens. Steam pours out. The fishing boats turn and run.

9.name: Nāga (Oreka)
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) / Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ancient; the deep riverbed, the slow power of water that has carved stone for millennia
9.environment: Deep Great River (the deepest, oldest stretches of the Great River -- the canyon-passages where the river runs through stone gorges hundreds of feet deep, the confluence-pools where major tributaries meet, and the sacred deep-holes that Pekakarlik rivermen mark on their charts with respect-symbols)
9.description: The river is deep here -- so deep the bottom vanishes into green-black shadow, and the current runs slow and heavy as though the water itself were old. The Pekakarlik rivermen cut their engines and let the barge drift. They have been quiet for the last mile, speaking only in hand-signs, and when you ask why, the barge-master points to the water and puts a finger to his lips. Something vast is coiled on the riverbed below you -- a serpentine shape forty feet long, its scales iridescent even in t...

9.name: Piasa
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) / Lady of the Sea (Water) -- riverine; the resonance of river-clay, deep-bank mud, and the slow patient erosion of stone by water over centuries
9.environment: Steppe-River Border (the river-crossings, fords, and bluff-lines where the Great River and its tributaries cut through the Eternal Steppe's edge -- specifically the transitional zone where open grassland meets river-valley, where travellers are funnelled through narrow crossing-points)
9.organization: Solitary (always -- piasa are aggressively territorial against their own kind)
9.description: The thing above the river is not a bird. You knew that before your eyes confirmed it -- the wingbeats are wrong, too heavy, displaced air moving in a pattern that no bird produces. It is a dragon, but not like the dragons the scholars describe: no chromatic scales, no metallic sheen, no alignment to the elemental framework your tutors taught you. It is the colour of river-clay and old blood, its wings are feathered like an eagle's rather than membraned like a drake's, its face carries antlers ...

9.name: Spirit Naga
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted; the resonance of water that has been claimed and twisted by an intelligence that uses the Lady's domain for its own purposes -- river-current directed by will rather than gravity, water that serves a master
9.environment: Corrupted Waterways (stretches of river, creek, or lake where a spirit naga has established territorial dominance -- the water itself changes, becoming sluggish, discoloured, and hostile to normal aquatic life; most commonly found along Great River tributaries and the smaller waterways of the Kinsweave and Deepwater Marches)
9.description: The waterway ahead is wrong. The current runs sluggishly, the colour is off -- a faint yellow-green tinge beneath the surface -- and the reeds along the bank are wilted despite adequate water. Something has corrupted this stretch of river, and the corruption has a voice. It speaks from the water: a woman's voice, cultured, persuasive, and utterly, chillingly reasonable, explaining why you should come closer, why you should listen, why resistance to the inevitable is wasteful. The voice belongs ...

8.name: Dire Tiger
flags: elemental_affinity:Earth/Water (the Deepwood hunter -- shadow, patience, and the water-courses that thread the forest floor)
8.environment: Deepwood (the densest interior of the Great Forests, along river-courses and near forest pools where large prey comes to drink)

8.name: Mamlambo
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of the Sea (Water) -- corrupted; stagnant, hungry, the patience of water that has forgotten how to flow
8.environment: River-Steppe Border (the wide, slow-moving river crossings where the Great River's tributaries meet the Eternal Steppe -- the fording-points, gravel shallows, and seasonal flood-pools used by steppe herds and Mytroan caravans)
8.description: The river crossing has been safe for a decade. The Warg packs ford here every season and the Mytroan riders use it as a watering-point on the steppe-circuit. Then the horses stopped drinking. The Wargs crossed upstream, three miles out of their way, and when asked why, the pack-leader said only: 'The river has teeth now.' You crouch at the bank and the water looks normal -- brown, shallow, running smooth over gravel. You cannot see what is lying on the bottom, because the thing on the bottom h...

8.name: Ōmukade
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- toxic; heavy, metallic earth-resonance with a chemical-burn quality, like copper ore exposed to acid
8.environment: Deep Forest (Tanuki groves and the surrounding old-growth interior of the Great Forests -- the densest, darkest forest-floor zones where fallen trees create tunnel-like passages and the canopy blocks all sunlight)
8.description: The centipede fills the tunnel. That is the first and most important fact -- it fills the tunnel from wall to wall, from floor to ceiling, and it is moving toward you with a sound like a thousand knives being drawn across stone simultaneously. It is the colour of dried blood, its body is as thick as a wine-barrel, and every segment carries a pair of legs that end in hooks sharp enough to gouge stone. Its head is a nightmare of mandibles and compound eyes, and when it opens its jaws, the air fi...

8.name: Treant
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- ancient forest-foundation; the resonance of the earth beneath a forest's oldest roots, soil that has been forest-floor for so long that the trees and the earth have become one system -- not air (though the canopy moves in wind) but earth, the foundation from which all growth proceeds
8.environment: Great Forests (the old-growth hearts of the Great Forests -- specifically the zones where trees have grown undisturbed for centuries, where the root-networks are deep and interconnected, and where the forest's elemental identity is strongest)
8.description: The oak in the clearing is old -- older than the settlement downstream, older than the Pasua patrol-routes that pass its roots, old enough that its trunk is wider than three people standing shoulder to shoulder. You have walked past it twice today. You did not notice the face. You notice it now because the face is looking at you, and the eyes -- formed from knots in the bark, framed by ridges that were not there this morning -- express a patience so vast and so slow that your entire life is, to ...

0.name: Kodama
flags: elemental_affinity:Lord of Stone (Earth) -- the deep, slow earth-resonance of a living tree's connection to the soil
0.environment: Great Forests (the interior of the oldest forests -- wherever a tree has stood long enough and grown deep enough to develop a kodama; never in young growth, plantations, or recently-cleared areas)
0.organization: Solitary (one per tree, always) or grove (3-20, one per tree in a kodama-grove)
0.description: The sound is not a sound. It is a vibration -- a resonance that comes from the tree itself, from the ring of wood inside the bark, from the sap moving through channels that have been open for five hundred years. If you press your ear to the trunk and listen with more than your ears, you hear it: a pulse, slow and patient, like a heartbeat that measures its time in seasons rather than seconds. The kodama is not in the tree. The kodama is the tree's awareness of itself.

10.name: Rakshasa
flags: elemental_affinity:None -- false; a rakshasa generates a convincing but artificial elemental resonance that matches whatever identity it is currently wearing, constructed through sheer force of will and centuries of practice; the false resonance is detectable as fake only on a DC 25 Kin-sense check
10.environment: Warm Settlements (the river-cities of the Eruskan, the oasis-towns of the Pekakarlik, the larger trading-posts of the Kinsweave border-kingdoms -- anywhere commerce creates wealth, complexity creates cover, and population density creates prey)
10.description: The merchant is perfect. His smile is warm, his voice is cultured, his robes are exactly the quality a man of his station would wear -- not ostentatious, not modest, the precise median. His hands are soft and well-manicured. His knowledge of the spice-trade is encyclopaedic. He has been a fixture of this Eruskan river-city for eleven years, and every person who knows him considers him reliable, generous, and utterly trustworthy. The only thing wrong with him -- the only thing -- is that his hand...

10.name: Half-Dómnathar Philosopher (Bard 8)
flags: half_domnathar
hostile
caster
boss
special_qualities: Lore Mastery: any Kn roll vs DC 20 is free info
10.environment: Twin Rivers (Unstrung Philosophers' Refuge in Riverwind Heights)
10.organization: solitary (teaching a class of 4-8 Unstrung cultists)
10.description: A speaker whose voice most people would listen to regardless of content. He teaches the Deceiver's Feat as philosophy -- 'the power others would prefer you did not have' -- and his students do not always know whose feet they are sitting at.

11.name: Zmey Gorynych
flags: elemental_affinity:Lady of Fire (Fire) -- volcanic intelligence; the resonance of fire at its most calculating, the Lady's domain in its predatory, thinking aspect -- not the mindless burn of a wildfire or the nurturing warmth of a forge-bond but the deliberate, patient, strategic heat of magma that chooses where to flow
11.environment: Scorchspires (Rarozhki) -- the deep volcanic valleys, caldera-rims, and magma-lake caverns of the Scorchspires' inner range -- specifically the zones too hot for any creature without fire immunity, where the zmey gorynych lairs in comfort while everything else is kept at bay by temperature alone
11.description: Three heads. That is what the Rarozhki scouts reported, and three heads is what you see: three serpentine necks rising from a body built like a fortress of red-black scales, each head armoured, horned, and breathing independently. The left head watches you. The centre head watches the sky. The right head watches the tunnel behind it. Nothing approaches a zmey gorynych from a single direction, because the zmey gorynych is watching every direction simultaneously. Each mouth trails smoke. Each e...
